quote: Please leave a message, and I will return your call as soon as I'm able.
stewart_read: The transcript captures the called party's outgoing voicemail greeting and then ends. Whether the rep left a voicemail message, hung up, or whether additional rep speech was simply not captured by the Deepgram pipeline cannot be determined from this record alone. This is a transcription-pipeline ambiguity — Deepgram may have captured the greeting before the beep and stopped recording before any rep message was left, or the rep may have disconnected. Manager verification against the dialer log or call recording is required before any coaching conclusion can be drawn about rep behavior on this touch.
alternative_hypothesis: The rep did leave a voicemail message that was not captured by the Deepgram pipeline due to a recording cutoff at the voicemail greeting — in which case no rep behavior gap exists on this touch.
coaching_implication: Before reviewing this call with the rep, pull the raw audio or dialer log to confirm whether a voicemail was left. If no message was left, the coaching conversation is about voicemail-touch protocol: Ion has no codex section that directly governs voicemail behavior, which is itself a gap worth flagging to Kenny. If a message was left but not captured, this is a Deepgram pipeline issue to route to the ops team, not a rep coaching moment.

quote: Please leave a message, and I will return your call as soon as I'm able.
stewart_read: The codex contains no section governing voicemail-touch protocol — what to say, whether to leave a message, how to frame the callback ask, or how to sequence a voicemail touch within a multi-touch cadence. This is a schema gap, not a rep gap. The absence of a voicemail protocol means every setter on the floor is improvising this moment without a standard.
coaching_implication: Flag to Kenny: the codex needs a voicemail-touch protocol section. At minimum it should cover: (1) whether to leave a message on a first touch vs. nth touch, (2) what a compliant voicemail sounds like for Tesla-sourced vs. organic leads, (3) how the text-after-call protocol (button_up.text_followup_promise) applies when no live contact was made. This is a floor-wide gap, not a per-rep coaching item.

quote: Please leave a message, and I will return your call as soon as I'm able.
stewart_read: On a paid lead, the bill is required before an appointment can be set. If this is a paid lead and the rep reached voicemail, the bill-collection gap is now a follow-up problem — there is no live call on which to collect it. The codex's anti-pattern detection note (bill_collection.stewart_automated_bill_capture) flags the risk of reps using personal phone numbers for bill follow-up after a missed live contact. That anti-pattern risk is live here if the rep attempts to recover this lead via personal outreach. Note: the capability spec's trigger ('setter clicks follow up for bill in Stewart UI at end of call') assumes a live call occurred; it does not directly apply to a voicemail-touch record.
coaching_implication: If this is a paid lead, the manager should confirm the lead's follow-up routing: is it re-queued for another dial attempt, or does the rep own the callback? If the rep owns it, reinforce that bill follow-up should not route through personal phone numbers. This is a process question for Kenny, not a rep-behavior coaching moment until lead type is confirmed.

quote: Please leave a message, and I will return your call as soon as I'm able.
stewart_read: A voicemail touch produces no sit and therefore no setter compensation under Ion's stair-step pay model. If this rep is mid-pay-period and near a tier threshold, a string of voicemail-only touches has compounding cost — not just in missed sits but in stalled tier progression. The transcript alone cannot tell us how many touches this lead has received or where the rep sits in their current pay period, but the compensation stakes make voicemail-touch efficiency a non-trivial coaching topic.
coaching_implication: Use this call as a prompt to check the rep's current pay-period tier position and touch count on this lead. If the rep is close to a tier step-up, the urgency of converting this lead to a live contact — and the cost of continued voicemail touches — is concrete and paycheck-relevant. This is the tier-aware coaching move the codex describes.
